keyPoints: Sender sends a request to the receiver to start the sliding window protocol.
Receiver sends an acknowledgment (ACK) to the sender if the data is received correctly.
Receiver sends a negative acknowledgment (Nak) to the sender if the data is not received correctly.
Sender adjusts the size of the sliding window based on the messages received from the receiver.
Sender sends data to the receiver using the current size of the sliding window.
Receiver waits for an acknowledgment (ACK) from the sender before sending the next data packet.
Sender and receiver continuously adjust the size of the sliding window to ensure efficient data transfer.
